title: Bubbles, Suds, and Squeaky Clean Fun!
story: Millions of people celebrate National Bubble Bath Day on January 8 by filling their tubs with warm water and mountains of fluffy bubbles! Kids and adults alike add special bubble bath soap, bath bombs, or even dish soap to create the perfect sudsy paradise. Some people bring rubber ducks, waterproof books, or snacks to make their soak extra special. Others take silly selfies with bubble beards or crowns to share online. This bubbly holiday reminds everyone to slow down and enjoy simple pleasures after the busy holiday season ends.
activity_question: What do people often bring into their bubble baths to make them more fun?
follow_up_question: What would be your dream bubble bath setup?

title: Sweet and Crunchy Toffee Day!
story: Crunch! That's the sound of people biting into delicious English toffee on January 8, which is National English Toffee Day! This sweet holiday celebrates the buttery, crunchy candy that's made by cooking sugar and butter until it hardens into golden perfection. People mark this tasty day by making homemade toffee in their kitchens, buying fancy toffee from candy shops, or sharing toffee treats with friends and family. Many bakers love to dip their toffee in chocolate and roll it in chopped nuts for extra yumminess!
activity_question: What two main ingredients are cooked together to make English toffee?
bonus_fact: English toffee actually became popular in America, not England!

title: Spread the JoyGerm Bug!
story: On January 8, people celebrate National JoyGerm Day by spreading happiness like it's contagious! The idea is simple: joy spreads from person to person just like germs do, but in the best possible way. People celebrate by doing random acts of kindness, sharing compliments, telling jokes, and giving unexpected hugs or high-fives. Some folks post cheerful messages on social media, leave encouraging notes for strangers to find, or surprise friends with small gifts. Teachers might organize classroom activities where students write positive messages to each other. The goal is to "infect" as many people as possible with good vibes and watch the happiness multiply throughout the day!
activity_question: According to the story, how does joy spread on National JoyGerm Day?
activity_choices: Like germs, from person to person
activity_answer: Like germs, from person to person
bonus_fact: Some people keep a "joy journal" to track all the happiness they spread!

title: The King's Birthday Rocks On
story: Fans around the world celebrate Elvis Presley's birthday on January 8 with dancing, singing, and lots of hip swiveling! People dress up in sparkly jumpsuits, slick back their hair, and practice their best "Thank you, thank you very much" impressions. Radio stations play his biggest hits like "Hound Dog" and "Can't Help Falling in Love" all day long. At Graceland, his famous mansion in Memphis, thousands of visitors gather to honor the King of Rock and Roll. Fans share videos of themselves doing Elvis dance moves on social media, and some even hold Elvis karaoke contests. It's a day when everyone can feel a little more rock and roll!
activity_question: What do Elvis fans often wear when celebrating his birthday?
bonus_fact: Elvis's middle name was actually spelled "Aron" but he later changed it to "Aaron."

title: Diamond Pattern Day!
story: Every January 8, people celebrate National Argyle Day by wearing those famous diamond-shaped patterns! You know argyle - those colorful diamond designs you see on socks, sweaters, and golf clothes. Today, fashion fans dig through their closets to find their most eye-catching argyle pieces. Some people wear argyle socks with shorts just to be silly, while others go all-out with argyle sweaters, vests, or even ties.
activity_question: What shape makes up the argyle pattern?
follow_up_question: What's the most unusual place you've seen an argyle pattern?

title: Spinning Into National Earth's Rotation Day
story: Every January 8, National Earth's Rotation Day celebrates our planet's amazing spinning motion! People share cool facts about how Earth spins once every 24 hours, creating day and night. Science teachers love using spinning globes and basketballs to show how rotation works. Kids post videos of themselves spinning like Earth on social media. It's the perfect day to appreciate that we're all passengers on a giant spinning rock zooming through space!
activity_question: How long does it take Earth to spin around once completely?
follow_up_question: What would happen if Earth suddenly stopped spinning?
